title: 2 Corinthians 5:17
message: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!
notes: Not the old version improved — a brand new creation. The old is not reformed or rehabilitated; it is gone. I do not carry forward what Christ has already ended.

title: Galatians 6:15
message: Neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything; what counts is the new creation.
notes: Religious status, ethnic identity, cultural background — none of it defines me now. The only thing that counts before God is what I have become in Christ. I am a new creation.

title: Ephesians 4:24
message: And to put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
notes: The new self is created in the likeness of God — not a distant aspiration, but my actual design. I was made new in righteousness and holiness. This is who I am, not who I am trying to become.

title: Colossians 3:10
message: And have put on the new self, which is being renewed in knowledge in the image of its Creator.
notes: The new self is being renewed in knowledge — progressively revealing the image of the Creator already embedded in me. The more I know God, the more of what I already am in Christ becomes visible.

title: Romans 6:4
message: We were therefore buried with him through baptism into death in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, we too may live a new life.
notes: My old life was buried with Christ — it did not survive the cross. The life I now live is a resurrection life, powered by the same glory of the Father that raised Jesus from the dead.

title: John 3:6
message: Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the Spirit gives birth to spirit.
notes: I have been born of the Spirit — my origin is now from above, not from below. I carry the nature of my new birth, not the limitations of my natural birth.

title: 1 Peter 1:23
message: For you have been born again, not of perishable seed, but of imperishable, through the living and enduring word of God.
notes: Imperishable seed — the life inside me cannot decay, cannot be corrupted, cannot die. My new nature is incorruptible because the Word of God that produced it endures forever.

title: Romans 8:29
message: For those God foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brothers and sisters.
notes: I am predestined to bear the image of Christ — conformity to His likeness is not optional or uncertain, it is God's predetermined purpose for me. I am being shaped toward something God already decided.

title: 2 Peter 1:4
message: Through these he has given us his very great and precious promises, so that through them you may participate in the divine nature, having escaped the corruption in the world caused by evil desires.
notes: Participation in the divine nature — I do not merely follow God's rules, I share His nature. The new creation is not moral reform; it is a genuine impartation of who God is into who I am.

title: Isaiah 43:18-19
message: Forget the former things; do not dwell on the past. See, I am doing a new thing! Now it springs up; do you not perceive it? I am making a way in the wilderness and streams in the desert.
notes: God commands me not to dwell on the former things — the old identity, the old failures, the old narrative. He is doing something new, and it is already springing up. My job is to perceive it and walk in it.
